Fast Wilcoxon Rank-Sum + AUC Marker Test (BPCells / Seurat-v5 Compatible)

RunWilcoxAUC.Rd

A drop-in replacement for presto::wilcoxauc(seurat_object, ...) that works reliably with BPCells-backed and Seurat v5 multi-layer objects.

The standard presto::wilcoxauc.Seurat method extracts the expression matrix internally in a way that can error with lazy matrices or v5 layer syntax. RunWilcoxAUC bypasses that path: it pulls the matrix through .get_layer_data() (which handles BPCells lazy subsetting, Seurat v3 slot= vs v5 layer=, and sparse → dgCMatrix coercion) and then calls presto::wilcoxauc(matrix, y) so S3 dispatch lands on wilcoxauc.default directly - no internal ::: access needed.

Usage

RunWilcoxAUC(
  seurat_object,
  group.by = NULL,
  groups_use = NULL,
  assay = "RNA",
  layer = "data",
  ...
)

Arguments

seurat_object

A Seurat object.

group.by

Character. Metadata column whose levels define the comparison groups (e.g. "Cluster", "Assignment"). When NULL, Idents is used.

groups_use

Character vector or NULL. Restrict the test to a subset of group.by levels. NULL = all levels.

assay

Character. Assay to extract. Default "RNA".

layer

Character. Layer / slot to extract. Default "data" (log-normalised counts, recommended for AUC statistics). "counts" uses raw counts; "scale.data" uses scaled values (note: negative values make AUC less interpretable but the test is still valid).

...

Additional arguments forwarded to wilcoxauc.

Value

A data.frame with one row per (feature × group) combination containing auc, pval, padj, logFC, pct_in, pct_out - the standard presto output.

See also

wilcoxauc

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage - use active identity
markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj)

# Specify a metadata column
markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj, group.by = "Cluster")

# Only compare specific groups
markers <- RunWilcoxAUC(SeuratObj, group.by = "Cluster",
                         groups_use = c("Microglia", "Astrocytes"))

# Top markers per cluster
library(dplyr)
top_markers <- markers |>
  filter(padj < 0.05, logFC > 0.5, auc > 0.6) |>
  group.by(group) |>
  slice_max(auc, n = 20)
} # }
